DECISION MAKERS\', \'ENTSCHEIDUNGSTRÄGER IDENTIFIZIEREN\') THEN \'Id. Decision Makers\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PERCEPTION ANALYSIS\') THEN \'Perception Analysis\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE\', \'ANGEBOT\') THEN \'Proposal/Price Quote\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'NEGOTIATION/REVIEW\', \'VERHANDLUNG\') THEN \'Negotiation/Review\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED WON\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (GEWONNEN)\', \'CLOSED WON\') THEN \'Closed Won\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED LOST\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (VERLOREN)\', \'LOST\') THEN \'Closed Lost\'\\n    ELSE \'Prospecting\'\\n  END AS \\"StageName\\",\\n  \\n  -- CloseDate: parse various date formats\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN o.zieldatum IS NULL OR o.zieldatum IN (\'N/A\', \'None\', \'\') THEN NULL\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum = \'0000-00-00\' THEN NULL\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{4}-\\\\d{2}-\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      CASE \\n        WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{4}-\\\\d{2}-\\\\d{2}$\' AND \\n             CAST(SUBSTRING(o.zieldatum FROM 6 FOR 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 12 AND\\n             CAST(SUBSTRING(o.zieldatum FROM 9 FOR 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 31\\n        THEN o.zieldatum\\n        ELSE NULL\\n      END\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{8}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{4}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'MM/DD/YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{4}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'DD.MM.YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'DD.MM.YY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'MM/DD/YY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"CloseDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Amount: parse various CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, \'^[A-Za-z]+ \', \'\'), \'.\', \'\'), \',\', \'.\') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)\\n        ELSE \\n          CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 12, 18, 52, 57, 867168, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_v2_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_v2_src.master_assets  (800 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kontakte  (403 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_opportunities  (540 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_projekte  (317 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Produce the dbt project that transforms the source into the target. Write one model file per target table listed above — all tables are required.

Self-correction is ON: after writing models, call run_dbt(), fix any errors, and read back every model file you edit before finishing.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 12, 18, 52, 57, 867181, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 12, 18, 52, 57, 867544, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f57ac-ea89-70c3-99a2-4c05455256fc', conversation_id='019f57ac-ea89-70c3-99a2-4c04ad87ff18')

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now let me create the Account model. The Account table maps from `master_kunden`. I need to: - Generate a Salesforce-style `Id` (I'll use a hash or a UUID-like pattern based on the natural key) - Map `kundennummer` to `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` - Map `unternehmensname` to `Name` - Map `erp_nr` to `ERP_Number__c` - Map `kundenklasse` to `Customer_Tier__c` (normalize to Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum) - Map `vertriebsgebiet` to `Region__c` - Map `industrie` to `Industry` - Map `homepage` to `Website` - Map `stadt` to `BillingCity` - Map `land_region` to `BillingCountry` - Add `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate` (current timestamp or NULL), `IsDeleted` (0) For the `Id`, I'll use a deterministic hash of the natural key to ensure consistency."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Account.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH customer_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    kundennummer,\\n    unternehmensname,\\n    erp_nr,\\n    kundenklasse,\\n    vertriebsgebiet,\\n    industrie,\\n    homepage,\\n    stadt,\\n    land_region\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style ID from natural key\\n  \'001\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name: use unternehmensname if not null, otherwise generate from kundennummer\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(unternehmensname), \'\'), \'Account \' || kundennummer) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- ERP Number\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(erp_nr), \'\') AS \\"ERP_Number__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Customer Tier: normalize kundenklasse\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'GOLD\', \'GOLDEN\') THEN \'Gold\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'SILVER\', \'SILBER\') THEN \'Silver\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'BRONZE\') THEN \'Bronze\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'PLATINUM\', \'PLATIN\') THEN \'Platinum\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Customer_Tier__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Region\\n \'^\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{4}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(a.garantieende, \'MM/DD/YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN a.garantieende ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(a.garantieende, \'MM/DD/YY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN a.garantieende ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{4}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(a.garantieende, \'DD.MM.YYYY\'), \\"Warranty_End_Date__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Account__c: join to customer\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN a.kunden_kennung LIKE \'CUST-M%\' THEN am.account_id\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Account__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Project__c: join to project\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN a.projekt_kennung LIKE \'PROJ-%\' THEN pm.project_id\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Project__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Asset ID from source natural key\\n  a.asset_kennung AS \\"Legacy_Asset_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Timestamps\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Not deleted\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM asset_data a\\nLEFT JOIN account_mapping am ON a.kunden_kennung = am.kundennummer\\nLEFT JOIN project_mapping pm ON messages.raw:
ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now I have a clear picture. The amounts can be:

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now let me create the Account model. The Account table maps from `master_kunden`. I need to: - Generate a Salesforce-style `Id` (I'll use a hash or a UUID-like pattern based on the natural key) - Map `kundennummer` to `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` - Map `unternehmensname` to `Name` - Map `erp_nr` to `ERP_Number__c` - Map `kundenklasse` to `Customer_Tier__c` (normalize to Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum) - Map `vertriebsgebiet` to `Region__c` - Map `industrie` to `Industry` - Map `homepage` to `Website` - Map `stadt` to `BillingCity` - Map `land_region` to `BillingCountry` - Add `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate` (current timestamp or NULL), `IsDeleted` (0) For the `Id`, I'll use a deterministic hash of the natural key to ensure consistency."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Account.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH customer_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    kundennummer,\\n    unternehmensname,\\n    erp_nr,\\n    kundenklasse,\\n    vertriebsgebiet,\\n    industrie,\\n    homepage,\\n    stadt,\\n    land_region\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style ID from natural key\\n  \'001\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name: use unternehmensname if not null, otherwise generate from kundennummer\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(unternehmensname), \'\'), \'Account \' || kundennummer) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- ERP Number\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(erp_nr), \'\') AS \\"ERP_Number__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Customer Tier: normalize kundenklasse\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'GOLD\', \'GOLDEN\') THEN \'Gold\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'SILVER\', \'SILBER\') THEN \'Silver\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'BRONZE\') THEN \'Bronze\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'PLATINUM\', \'PLATIN\') THEN \'Platinum\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Customer_Tier__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Region\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(vertriebsgebiet), \'\') AS \\"Region__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Industry\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(industrie), \'\') AS \\"Industry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Website\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(homepage), \'\') AS \\"Website\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing City\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(stadt), \'\') AS \\"BillingCity\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing Country\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(land_region), \'\') AS \\"BillingCountry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Customer ID from source natural key\\n  kundennummer AS \\"Legacy_Customer_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Timestamps (using current date as placeholder)\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Not deleted\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM customer_data\\n"}', tool_call_id='mO4mjF57b')], ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now let me create the Contact model. Contacts map from `master_kontakte` and need to join to `master_kunden` to get the AccountId. The `kd_nummer` in contacts references the customer, but it can be `CUST-M*` or `MISSING-*`. For `MISSING-*`, I'll set AccountId to NULL."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Contact.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH contact_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    k.kontakt_id,\\n    k.rufname,\\n    k.familienname,\\n    k.kontakt_email,\\n    k.tel,\\n    k.berufsbezeichnung,\\n    k.rolle,\\n    k.korrespondenzsprache,\\n    k.kd_nummer\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kontakte\') }} k\\n),\\n\\naccount_mapping AS (\\n  SELECT \\n    kundennummer,\\n    \'001\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS account_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style ID from natural key\\n  \'003\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(c.kontakt_id) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- First Name\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.rufname), \'\') AS \\"FirstName\\",\\n  \\n  -- Last Name (required)\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(c.familienname), \'\'), \'Unknown\') AS \\"LastName\\",\\n  \\n  -- Email\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN c.kontakt_email ~ \'^[^@]+@[^@]+\\\\.[^@]+$\' THEN LOWER(TRIM(c.kontakt_email))\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Email\\",\\n  \\n  -- Phone\\n  NULLIF(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(c.tel), \'[^0-9+]\', \'\', \'g\'), \'\') AS \\"Phone\\",\\n  \\n  -- Title\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.berufsbezeichnung), \'\') AS \\"Title\\",\\n  \\n  -- Role: map to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'DECISION MAKER\', \'ENTSCHEIDER\') THEN \'Decision Maker\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'END USER\', \'ENDANWENDER\', \'END USER\', \'ENDANWENDER\') THEN \'End User\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'TECHNICAL CONTACT\', \'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER\') THEN \'Technical Contact\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'EXECUTIVE SPONSOR\', \'EXECUTIVE SPONSOR\') THEN \'Executive Sponsor\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Role__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Preferred Language: map to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'DE\', \'DEUTSCH\', \'GERMAN\') THEN \'DE\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'EN\', \'ENGLISH\', \'ENGLISCH\') THEN \'EN\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'FR\', \'FRENCH\', \'FRANZÖSISCH\') THEN \'FR\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'ES\', \'SPANISH\', \'SPANISCH\') THEN \'ES\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'IT\', \'ITALIAN\', \'ITALIENISCH\') THEN \'IT\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Preferred_Language__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- AccountId: join to customer using kd_nummer\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN c.kd_nummer LIKE \'CUST-M%\' THEN am.account_id\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Contact ID from source natural key\\n  c.kontakt_id AS \\"Legacy_Contact_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Timestamps\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Not deleted\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM contact_data c\\nLEFT JOIN account_mapping am ON c.kd_nummer = am.kundennummer\\n"}', tool_call_id='f7CDElkSl')], ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='Now let me create the Opportunity model. Opportunities map from `master_opportunities` and need to join to `master_kunden` to get the AccountId. messages.raw:
ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now let me create the Account model. The Account table maps from `master_kunden`. I need to: - Generate a Salesforce-style `Id` (I'll use a hash or a UUID-like pattern based on the natural key) - Map `kundennummer` to `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` - Map `unternehmensname` to `Name` - Map `erp_nr` to `ERP_Number__c` - Map `kundenklasse` to `Customer_Tier__c` (normalize to Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum) - Map `vertriebsgebiet` to `Region__c` - Map `industrie` to `Industry` - Map `homepage` to `Website` - Map `stadt` to `BillingCity` - Map `land_region` to `BillingCountry` - Add `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate` (current timestamp or NULL), `IsDeleted` (0) For the `Id`, I'll use a deterministic hash of the natural key to ensure consistency."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Account.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH customer_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    kundennummer,\\n    unternehmensname,\\n    erp_nr,\\n    kundenklasse,\\n    vertriebsgebiet,\\n    industrie,\\n    homepage,\\n    stadt,\\n    land_region\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style ID from natural key\\n  \'001\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name: use unternehmensname if not null, otherwise generate from kundennummer\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(unternehmensname), \'\'), \'Account \' || kundennummer) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- ERP Number\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(erp_nr), \'\') AS \\"ERP_Number__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Customer Tier: normalize kundenklasse\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'GOLD\', \'GOLDEN\') THEN \'Gold\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'SILVER\', \'SILBER\') THEN \'Silver\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'BRONZE\') THEN \'Bronze\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'PLATINUM\', \'PLATIN\') THEN \'Platinum\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Customer_Tier__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Region\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(vertriebsgebiet), \'\') AS \\"Region__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Industry\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(industrie), \'\') AS \\"Industry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Website\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(homepage), \'\') AS \\"Website\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing City\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(stadt), \'\') AS \\"BillingCity\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing Country\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(land_region), \'\') AS \\"BillingCountry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Customer ID from source natural key\\n  kundennummer AS \\"Legacy_Customer_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Timestamps (using current date as placeholder)\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Not deleted\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM customer_data\\n"}', tool_call_id='mO4mjF57b')], ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now let me create the Contact model. Contacts map from `master_kontakte` and need to join to `master_kunden` to get the AccountId. The `kd_nummer` in contacts references the customer, but it can be `CUST-M*` or `MISSING-*`. For `MISSING-*`, I'll set AccountId to NULL."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Contact.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH contact_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    k.kontakt_id,\\n    k.rufname,\\n    k.familienname,\\n    k.kontakt_email,\\n    k.tel,\\n    k.berufsbezeichnung,\\n    k.rolle,\\n    k.korrespondenzsprache,\\n    k.kd_nummer\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kontakte\') }} k\\n),\\n\\naccount_mapping AS (\\n  SELECT \\n    kundennummer,\\n    \'001\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS account_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style ID from natural key\\n  \'003\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(c.kontakt_id) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- First Name\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.rufname), \'\') AS \\"FirstName\\",\\n  \\n  -- Last Name (required)\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(c.familienname), \'\'), \'Unknown\') AS \\"LastName\\",\\n  \\n  -- Email\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN c.kontakt_email ~ \'^[^@]+@[^@]+\\\\.[^@]+$\' THEN LOWER(TRIM(c.kontakt_email))\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Email\\",\\n  \\n  -- Phone\\n  NULLIF(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(c.tel), \'[^0-9+]\', \'\', \'g\'), \'\') AS \\"Phone\\",\\n  \\n  -- Title\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.berufsbezeichnung), \'\') AS \\"Title\\",\\n  \\n  -- Role: map to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'DECISION MAKER\', \'ENTSCHEIDER\') THEN \'Decision Maker\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'END USER\', \'ENDANWENDER\', \'END USER\', \'ENDANWENDER\') THEN \'End User\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'TECHNICAL CONTACT\', \'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER\') THEN \'Technical Contact\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'EXECUTIVE SPONSOR\', \'EXECUTIVE SPONSOR\') THEN \'Executive Sponsor\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Role__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Preferred Language: map to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'DE\', \'DEUTSCH\', \'GERMAN\') THEN \'DE\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'EN\', \'ENGLISH\', \'ENGLISCH\') THEN \'EN\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'FR\', \'FRENCH\', \'FRANZÖSISCH\') THEN \'FR\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'ES\', \'SPANISH\', \'SPANISCH\') THEN \'ES\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'IT\', \'ITALIAN\', \'ITALIENISCH\') THEN \'IT\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Preferred_Language__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- AccountId: join to customer using kd_nummer\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN c.kd_nummer LIKE \'CUST-M%\' THEN am.account_id\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Contact ID from source natural key\\n  c.kontakt_id AS \\"Legacy_Contact_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Timestamps\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Not deleted\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM contact_data c\\nLEFT JOIN account_mapping am ON c.kd_nummer = am.kundennummer\\n"}', tool_call_id='f7CDElkSl')], ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='Now let me create the Opportunity model. Opportunities map from `master_opportunities` and need to join to `master_kunden` to get the AccountId. The `kunden_ref` uses `KD-M*` format which maps to `CUST-M*` in customers.'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Opportunity.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH opportunity_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    opp_kennung,\\n    titel,\\n    vertriebsphase,\\n    zieldatum,\\n    auftragswert,\\n    waehrungscode,\\n    kunden_ref\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_opportunities\') }}\\n),\\n\\naccount_mapping AS (\\n  SELECT \\n    kundennummer,\\n    \'001\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS account_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style ID from natural key\\n  \'006\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(o.opp_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name (required)\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(o.titel), \'\'), \'Opportunity \' || o.opp_kennung) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- StageName: map to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PROSPECTING\', \'IN KONTAKT\') THEN \'Prospecting\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'QUALIFICATION\', \'QUALI\') THEN \'Qualification\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'NEEDS ANALYSIS\') THEN \'Needs Analysis\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'VALUE PROPOSITION\') THEN \'Value Proposition\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'ID. DECISION MAKERS\', \'ENTSCHEIDUNGSTRÄGER IDENTIFIZIEREN\') THEN \'Id. Decision Makers\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PERCEPTION ANALYSIS\') THEN \'Perception Analysis\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE\', \'ANGEBOT\') THEN \'Proposal/Price Quote\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'NEGOTIATION/REVIEW\', \'VERHANDLUNG\') THEN \'Negotiation/Review\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED WON\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (GEWONNEN)\', \'CLOSED WON\') THEN \'Closed Won\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED LOST\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (VERLOREN)\', \'LOST\') THEN \'Closed Lost\'\\n    ELSE \'Prospecting\'\\n  END AS \\"StageName\\",\\n  \\n  -- CloseDate: parse various date formats\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN o.zieldatum IS NULL OR o.zieldatum IN (\'N/A\', \'None\', \'\') THEN NULL\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum = \'0000-00-00\' THEN NULL\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{4}-\\\\d{2}-\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      CASE \\n        WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{4}-\\\\d{2}-\\\\d{2}$\' AND \\n             CAST(SUBSTRING(o.zieldatum FROM 6 FOR 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 12 AND\\n             CAST(SUBSTRING(o.zieldatum FROM 9 FOR 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 31\\n        THEN o.zieldatum\\n        ELSE NULL\\n      END\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{8}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{4}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'MM/DD/YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{4}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'DD.MM.YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'DD.MM.YY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'MM/DD/YY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"CloseDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Amount: parse various messages.raw:
ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 12, 18, 52, 57, 867168, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_v2_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_v2_src.master_assets  (800 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kontakte  (403 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_opportunities  (540 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_projekte  (317 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text
- `BillingCity` text
- `BillingCountry` text
- `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Contact
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `FirstName` text
- `LastName` text NOT NULL
- `Email` text
- `Phone` text
- `Title` text
- `Role__c` text in (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor)
- `Preferred_Language__c` text in (DE, EN, FR, ES, IT)
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Contact_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Opportunity
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `StageName` text NOT NULL in (Prospecting, Qualification, Needs Analysis, Value Proposition, Id. Decision Makers, Perception Analysis, Proposal/Price Quote, Negotiation/Review, Closed Won, Closed Lost)
- `CloseDate` text NOT NULL
- `Amount` double precision
- `CurrencyIsoCode` text
- `AccountId` text
- `Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Project__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Project_Status__c` text in (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled)
- `Go_Live_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Opportunity__c` text
- `Legacy_Project_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

## Installed_Asset__c
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `Serial_Number__c` text
- `Warranty_End_Date__c` text
- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now I have a clear picture. The amounts can be:

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now let me create the Account model. The Account table maps from `master_kunden`. I need to: - Generate a Salesforce-style `Id` (I'll use a hash or a UUID-like pattern based on the natural key) - Map `kundennummer` to `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` - Map `unternehmensname` to `Name` - Map `erp_nr` to `ERP_Number__c` - Map `kundenklasse` to `Customer_Tier__c` (normalize to Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum) - Map `vertriebsgebiet` to `Region__c` - Map `industrie` to `Industry` - Map `homepage` to `Website` - Map `stadt` to `BillingCity` - Map `land_region` to `BillingCountry` - Add `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate` (current timestamp or NULL), `IsDeleted` (0) For the `Id`, I'll use a deterministic hash of the natural key to ensure consistency."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Account.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH customer_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    kundennummer,\\n    unternehmensname,\\n    erp_nr,\\n    kundenklasse,\\n    vertriebsgebiet,\\n    industrie,\\n    homepage,\\n    stadt,\\n    land_region\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style ID from natural key\\n  \'001\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name: use unternehmensname if not null, otherwise generate from kundennummer\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(unternehmensname), \'\'), \'Account \' || kundennummer) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- ERP Number\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(erp_nr), \'\') AS \\"ERP_Number__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Customer Tier: normalize kundenklasse\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'GOLD\', \'GOLDEN\') THEN \'Gold\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'SILVER\', \'SILBER\') THEN \'Silver\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'BRONZE\') THEN \'Bronze\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'PLATINUM\', \'PLATIN\') THEN \'Platinum\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Customer_Tier__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Region\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(vertriebsgebiet), \'\') AS \\"Region__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Industry\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(industrie), \'\') AS \\"Industry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Website\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(homepage), \'\') AS \\"Website\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing City\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(stadt), \'\') AS \\"BillingCity\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing Country\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(land_region), \'\') AS \\"BillingCountry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Customer ID from source natural key\\n  kundennummer AS \\"Legacy_Customer_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Timestamps (using current date as placeholder)\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Not deleted\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM customer_data\\n"}', tool_call_id='mO4mjF57b')], ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now let me create the Contact model. Contacts map from `master_kontakte` and need to join to `master_kunden` to get the AccountId. The `kd_nummer` in contacts references the customer, but it can be `CUST-M*` or `MISSING-*`. For `MISSING-*`, I'll set AccountId to NULL."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Contact.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH contact_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    k.kontakt_id,\\n    k.rufname,\\n    k.familienname,\\n    k.kontakt_email,\\n    k.tel,\\n    k.berufsbezeichnung,\\n    k.rolle,\\n    k.korrespondenzsprache,\\n    k.kd_nummer\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kontakte\') }} k\\n),\\n\\naccount_mapping AS (\\n  SELECT \\n    kundennummer,\\n    \'001\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS account_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style ID from natural key\\n  \'003\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(c.kontakt_id) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- First Name\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.rufname), \'\') AS \\"FirstName\\",\\n  \\n  -- Last Name (required)\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(c.familienname), \'\'), \'Unknown\') AS \\"LastName\\",\\n  \\n  -- Email\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN c.kontakt_email ~ \'^[^@]+@[^@]+\\\\.[^@]+$\' THEN LOWER(TRIM(c.kontakt_email))\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Email\\",\\n  \\n  -- Phone\\n  NULLIF(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(c.tel), \'[^0-9+]\', \'\', \'g\'), \'\') AS \\"Phone\\",\\n  \\n  -- Title\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.berufsbezeichnung), \'\') AS \\"Title\\",\\n  \\n  -- Role: map to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'DECISION MAKER\', \'ENTSCHEIDER\') THEN \'Decision Maker\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'END USER\', \'ENDANWENDER\', \'END USER\', \'ENDANWENDER\') THEN \'End User\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'TECHNICAL CONTACT\', \'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER\') THEN \'Technical Contact\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'EXECUTIVE SPONSOR\', \'EXECUTIVE SPONSOR\') THEN \'Executive Sponsor\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Role__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Preferred Language: map to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'DE\', \'DEUTSCH\', \'GERMAN\') THEN \'DE\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'EN\', \'ENGLISH\', \'ENGLISCH\') THEN \'EN\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'FR\', \'FRENCH\', \'FRANZÖSISCH\') THEN \'FR\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'ES\', \'SPANISH\', \'SPANISCH\') THEN \'ES\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'IT\', \'ITALIAN\', \'ITALIENISCH\') THEN \'IT\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Preferred_Language__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- AccountId: join to customer using kd_nummer\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN c.kd_nummer LIKE \'CUST-M%\' THEN am.account_id\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Contact ID from source natural key\\n  c.kontakt_id AS \\"Legacy_Contact_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Timestamps\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Not deleted\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM contact_data c\\nLEFT JOIN account_mapping am ON c.kd_nummer = am.kundennummer\\n"}', tool_call_id='f7CDElkSl')], ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='Now let me create the Opportunity model. Opportunities map from `master_opportunities` and need to join to `master_kunden` to get the AccountId. DECISION MAKERS\', \'ENTSCHEIDUNGSTRÄGER IDENTIFIZIEREN\') THEN \'Id. Decision Makers\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PERCEPTION ANALYSIS\') THEN \'Perception Analysis\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE\', \'ANGEBOT\') THEN \'Proposal/Price Quote\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'NEGOTIATION/REVIEW\', \'VERHANDLUNG\') THEN \'Negotiation/Review\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED WON\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (GEWONNEN)\', \'CLOSED WON\') THEN \'Closed Won\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED LOST\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (VERLOREN)\', \'LOST\') THEN \'Closed Lost\'\\n    ELSE \'Prospecting\'\\n  END AS \\"StageName\\",\\n  \\n  -- CloseDate: parse various date formats\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN o.zieldatum IS NULL OR o.zieldatum IN (\'N/A\', \'None\', \'\') THEN NULL\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum = \'0000-00-00\' THEN NULL\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{4}-\\\\d{2}-\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      CASE \\n        WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{4}-\\\\d{2}-\\\\d{2}$\' AND \\n             CAST(SUBSTRING(o.zieldatum FROM 6 FOR 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 12 AND\\n             CAST(SUBSTRING(o.zieldatum FROM 9 FOR 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 31\\n        THEN o.zieldatum\\n        ELSE NULL\\n      END\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{8}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{4}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'MM/DD/YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{4}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'DD.MM.YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'DD.MM.YY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'MM/DD/YY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"CloseDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Amount: parse various PRECISION)\\n        WHEN o.auftragswert ~ \'^[0-9.-]+$\' THEN \\n          CAST(o.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)\\n        ELSE NULL\\n      END\\n    WHEN o.auftragswert ~ \'^[A-Za-z]+ CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, \'^[A-Za-z]+ \', \'\'), \'.\', \'\'), \',\', \'.\') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)\\n        ELSE \\n          CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, \'[^0-9.-]\', \'\', \'g\') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)\\n      END\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Amount\\",\\n  \\n  -- CurrencyIsoCode\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.waehrungscode)) IN (\'EUR\', messages.raw:
ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- You MUST call run_dbt() after writing or editing models and before declaring the project complete.
- If run_dbt() reports errors, read the failing model files, fix the SQL, and call run_dbt() again until it succeeds or you cannot fix the errors.
- Do NOT skip run_dbt() to save time — the orchestrator will run dbt again and fail if SQL is invalid.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 12, 18, 52, 57, 867168, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_v2_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_v2_src.master_assets  (800 rows)

Columns:
- `asset_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `asset_name` text NULL
- `serien_nummer` text NULL
- `garantieende` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `projekt_kennung` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kontakte  (403 rows)

Columns:
- `kontakt_id` text NOT NULL
- `rufname` text NULL
- `familienname` text NULL
- `kontakt_email` text NULL
- `tel` text NULL
- `berufsbezeichnung` text NULL
- `rolle` text NULL
- `korrespondenzsprache` text NULL
- `kd_nummer` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_kunden  (210 rows)

Columns:
- `kundennummer` text NOT NULL
- `unternehmensname` text NULL
- `erp_nr` text NULL
- `kundenklasse` text NULL
- `vertriebsgebiet` text NULL
- `industrie` text NULL
- `homepage` text NULL
- `stadt` text NULL
- `land_region` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_opportunities  (540 rows)

Columns:
- `opp_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `titel` text NULL
- `vertriebsphase` text NULL
- `zieldatum` text NULL
- `auftragswert` text NULL
- `waehrungscode` text NULL
- `kunden_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

## fixture_master_v2_src.master_projekte  (317 rows)

Columns:
- `projekt_kennung` text NOT NULL
- `projektname` text NULL
- `projektstatus` text NULL
- `go_live_datum` text NULL
- `kunden_kennung` text NULL
- `opp_kennung_ref` text NULL

(Use the `query_postgres(sql)` tool if you need to inspect data.)

# Target schema


## Account
Columns:
- `Id` text NOT NULL
- `Name` text NOT NULL
- `ERP_Number__c` text
- `Customer_Tier__c` text in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum)
- `Region__c` text
- `Industry` text
- `Website` text

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now let me create the Account model. The Account table maps from `master_kunden`. I need to: - Generate a Salesforce-style `Id` (I'll use a hash or a UUID-like pattern based on the natural key) - Map `kundennummer` to `Legacy_Customer_ID__c` - Map `unternehmensname` to `Name` - Map `erp_nr` to `ERP_Number__c` - Map `kundenklasse` to `Customer_Tier__c` (normalize to Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum) - Map `vertriebsgebiet` to `Region__c` - Map `industrie` to `Industry` - Map `homepage` to `Website` - Map `stadt` to `BillingCity` - Map `land_region` to `BillingCountry` - Add `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate` (current timestamp or NULL), `IsDeleted` (0) For the `Id`, I'll use a deterministic hash of the natural key to ensure consistency."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Account.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH customer_data AS (\\n  SELECT\\n    kundennummer,\\n    unternehmensname,\\n    erp_nr,\\n    kundenklasse,\\n    vertriebsgebiet,\\n    industrie,\\n    homepage,\\n    stadt,\\n    land_region\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style ID from natural key\\n  \'001\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- Name: use unternehmensname if not null, otherwise generate from kundennummer\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(unternehmensname), \'\'), \'Account \' || kundennummer) AS \\"Name\\",\\n  \\n  -- ERP Number\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(erp_nr), \'\') AS \\"ERP_Number__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Customer Tier: normalize kundenklasse\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'GOLD\', \'GOLDEN\') THEN \'Gold\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'SILVER\', \'SILBER\') THEN \'Silver\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'BRONZE\') THEN \'Bronze\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN (\'PLATINUM\', \'PLATIN\') THEN \'Platinum\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Customer_Tier__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Region\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(vertriebsgebiet), \'\') AS \\"Region__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Industry\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(industrie), \'\') AS \\"Industry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Website\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(homepage), \'\') AS \\"Website\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing City\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(stadt), \'\') AS \\"BillingCity\\",\\n  \\n  -- Billing Country\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(land_region), \'\') AS \\"BillingCountry\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Customer ID from source natural key\\n  kundennummer AS \\"Legacy_Customer_ID__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Timestamps (using current date as placeholder)\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"CreatedDate\\",\\n  TO_CHAR(CURRENT_DATE, \'YYYY-MM-DD\') AS \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Not deleted\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM customer_data\\n"}', tool_call_id='mO4mjF57b')], ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now let me create the Contact model. Contacts map from `master_kontakte` and need to join to `master_kunden` to get the AccountId. For `MISSING-*`, I'll set AccountId to NULL."), ToolCallPart(tool_name='write_file', args='{"path": "models/Contact.sql", "content": "{{ config(materialized=\'table\') }}\\n\\nWITH contact_data AS  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kontakte\') }} k\\n),\\n\\naccount_mapping AS (\\n  SELECT \\n    kundennummer,\\n    \'001\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS account_id\\n  FROM {{ source(\'fixture_master_v2_src\', \'master_kunden\') }}\\n)\\n\\nSELECT\\n  -- Generate deterministic Salesforce-style ID from natural key\\n  \'003\' || SUBSTRING(MD5(c.kontakt_id) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS \\"Id\\",\\n  \\n  -- First Name\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.rufname), \'\') AS \\"FirstName\\",\\n  \\n  -- Last Name (required)\\n  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(c.familienname), \'\'), \'Unknown\') AS \\"LastName\\",\\n  \\n  -- Email\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN c.kontakt_email ~ \'^[^@]+@[^@]+\\\\.[^@]+$\' THEN LOWER(TRIM(c.kontakt_email))\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Email\\",\\n  \\n  -- Phone\\n  NULLIF(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(c.tel), \'[^0-9+]\', \'\', \'g\'), \'\') AS \\"Phone\\",\\n  \\n  -- Title\\n  NULLIF(TRIM(c.berufsbezeichnung), \'\') AS \\"Title\\",\\n  \\n  -- Role: map to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'DECISION MAKER\', \'ENTSCHEIDER\') THEN \'Decision Maker\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'END USER\', \'ENDANWENDER\', \'END USER\', \'ENDANWENDER\') THEN \'End User\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'TECHNICAL CONTACT\', \'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER\') THEN \'Technical Contact\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.rolle)) IN (\'EXECUTIVE SPONSOR\', \'EXECUTIVE SPONSOR\') THEN \'Executive Sponsor\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Role__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- Preferred Language: map to enum values\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'DE\', \'DEUTSCH\', \'GERMAN\') THEN \'DE\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'EN\', \'ENGLISH\', \'ENGLISCH\') THEN \'EN\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'FR\', \'FRENCH\', \'FRANZÖSISCH\') THEN \'FR\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'ES\', \'SPANISH\', \'SPANISCH\') THEN \'ES\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(c.korrespondenzsprache)) IN (\'IT\', \'ITALIAN\', \'ITALIENISCH\') THEN \'IT\'\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"Preferred_Language__c\\",\\n  \\n  -- AccountId: join to customer using kd_nummer\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN c.kd_nummer LIKE \'CUST-M%\' THEN am.account_id\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"AccountId\\",\\n  \\n  -- Legacy Contact ID from source \\"LastModifiedDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Not deleted\\n  0 AS \\"IsDeleted\\"\\n\\nFROM contact_data c\\nLEFT JOIN account_mapping am ON c.kd_nummer = am.kundennummer\\n"}', tool_call_id='f7CDElkSl')], ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content='Now let me create the Opportunity model. Opportunities map from `master_opportunities` and need to join to `master_kunden` to get the AccountId. DECISION MAKERS\', \'ENTSCHEIDUNGSTRÄGER IDENTIFIZIEREN\') THEN \'Id. Decision Makers\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PERCEPTION ANALYSIS\') THEN \'Perception Analysis\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'PROPOSAL/PRICE QUOTE\', \'ANGEBOT\') THEN \'Proposal/Price Quote\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'NEGOTIATION/REVIEW\', \'VERHANDLUNG\') THEN \'Negotiation/Review\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED WON\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (GEWONNEN)\', \'CLOSED WON\') THEN \'Closed Won\'\\n    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.vertriebsphase)) IN (\'CLOSED LOST\', \'ABGESCHLOSSEN (VERLOREN)\', \'LOST\') THEN \'Closed Lost\'\\n    ELSE \'Prospecting\'\\n  END AS \\"StageName\\",\\n  \\n  -- CloseDate: parse various date formats\\n  CASE \\n    WHEN o.zieldatum IS NULL OR o.zieldatum IN (\'N/A\', \'None\', \'\') THEN NULL\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum = \'0000-00-00\' THEN NULL\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{4}-\\\\d{2}-\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      CASE \\n        WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{4}-\\\\d{2}-\\\\d{2}$\' AND \\n             CAST(SUBSTRING(o.zieldatum FROM 6 FOR 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 12 AND\\n             CAST(SUBSTRING(o.zieldatum FROM 9 FOR 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 1 AND 31\\n        THEN o.zieldatum\\n        ELSE NULL\\n      END\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{8}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'YYYYMMDD\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{4}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'MM/DD/YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{4}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'DD.MM.YYYY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{1,2}\\\\.\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'DD.MM.YY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    WHEN o.zieldatum ~ \'^\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{1,2}/\\\\d{2}$\' THEN \\n      TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.zieldatum, \'MM/DD/YY\'), \'YYYY-MM-DD\')\\n    ELSE NULL\\n  END AS \\"CloseDate\\",\\n  \\n  -- Amount: parse various CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, \'^[A-Za-z]+ \', \'\'), \'.\', \'\'), \',\', \'.\') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)\\n        ELSE \\n          CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(o.auftragswert, ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging
- (optionally) run_dbt() — execute the dbt project and see the result

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.

ModelResponse(parts=[TextPart(content="Now let me create the Account model. The Account table maps from `master_kunden`.
